text: The darkness and the stark, industrial qualities of this place evoke a sense of solitude in me. The dim light filters through glass, revealing only abstract hints of purpose—screens, signs, safety equipment, but seldom people. I wonder who else comes here, and if these empty chairs and lonely corridors ever witness moments of insight or only the passage of time. In this state of near silence, my thoughts echo, seeking meaning amidst the utilitarian design. I feel both curiosity and slight unease, as if I have entered a space meant more for function than for comfort.
